extractions.problem: Building whole-cell computational models to predict cellular phenotypes and accelerate biological discovery.
Assembling the large and diverse array of data needed for whole-cell modeling remains a major challenge due to scattered data organization.
extractions.method: Review and analysis of data centralization strategies, proposing a central data warehouse for whole-cell modeling.
extractions.finding: A central data warehouse can overcome challenges of scattered data to enable more predictive whole-cell models, requiring community collaboration.

extractions.problem: Estimating half-cell thermodynamic parameters from whole-cell Li-ion battery experiments with low error.
extractions.method: Multi-species, multi-reaction (MSMR) half-cell thermodynamic model adapted to whole-cell cycling, with sequential least-square optimization and bootstrap analysis.
extractions.finding: Low-error fits achieved for open-circuit voltage and differential voltage curves; identified specific MSMR insertion reactions responsible for capacity loss across 600 cell cycles.
extractions.baseline: Literature reference MSMR half-cell parameters (1-DOF fit)
extractions.result: Under 5 mV MAE for charge/discharge curves

extractions.problem: Integrated whole-cell geometric modeling of organelle interactions in S. cerevisiae.
Estimating half-cell thermodynamic parameters from whole-cell Li-ion battery experiments with low error.
extractions.method: Multi-species, multi-reaction (MSMR) half-cell thermodynamic model adapted to whole-cell cycling, with sequential least-square optimization and bootstrap analysis.
extractions.finding: Low-error fits achieved for open-circuit voltage and differential voltage curves; identified specific MSMR insertion reactions responsible for capacity loss across 600 cell cycles.
extractions.baseline: Literature reference MSMR half-cell parameters (1-DOF fit)
extractions.result: Under 5 mV MAE for charge/discharge curves

extractions.problem: No models of E. coli peptidoglycan (PG) have been constructed on the whole-cell scale with full compositional heterogeneity.
extractions.method: Multi-scale coarse-grained modeling (one CG bead per glycan), stochastic simulation of cross-linking, and PG_maker code for whole-cell PG construction.
extractions.finding: Built whole-cell PG models consistent with experimental data; pore size distributions showed surprisingly good agreement with experiments; analyzed effects of Braun's lipoprotein on periplasmic protein diffusion.
extractions.metric: Pore size distribution agreement with experimental estimates
extractions.result: Whole-cell PG models built in under an hour; pore sizes in good agreement with experimental estimates

extractions.problem: Investigating the impact of gene expression strategies in near whole-cell models of minimal bacteria.
Whole-cell models of bacteria lack replication of genetic material, the most fundamental process of life.
extractions.method: Brownian dynamics polymer simulation of replicating chromosomes at 10 bp resolution, with loop extrusion by SMC complexes and topoisomerase action, augmented by cryo-electron tomogram-derived ribosome distributions.
extractions.finding: Extended an existing whole-cell model of JCVI-syn3A to cover the entire cell cycle by modeling chromosome replication, organization changes, and multi-fork replication states.

extractions.problem: Establishing an ex vivo tool to quantify lens epithelial cell viability on whole porcine lenses for cytotoxicity studies.
extractions.method: Triple Hoechst-Ethidium-Calcein-AM (HEC) staining on whole porcine lenses with Staurosporine (STS) chemical toxicity model; macroscope imaging and ImageJ analysis.
extractions.finding: Triple HEC staining detected subtle mortality differences on whole lenses, confirming sensitivity for cytotoxicity studies of therapeutics targeting the lens.
extractions.result: STS: 3804 cells/mm² vs control: 3896 cells/mm², p=0.002

extractions.problem: Establishing an ex vivo tool to quantify lens epithelial cell viability on whole porcine lenses for cytotoxicity studies.
extractions.method: Triple Hoechst-Ethidium-Calcein-AM (HEC) staining on whole porcine lenses with Staurosporine (STS) chemical toxicity model; macroscope imaging and ImageJ analysis.
extractions.finding: Triple HEC staining detected subtle mortality differences on whole lenses, confirming sensitivity for cytotoxicity studies of therapeutics targeting the lens.
extractions.result: STS: 3804 cells/mm² vs control: 3896 cells/mm², p=0.002

extractions.problem: Determining thermodynamically accurate kinetic parameters for whole-cell reaction models.
Developing a hybrid stochastic/deterministic whole-cell model of the minimal cell JCVI-syn3A.
Investigating region- and cell-specific imbalances in inhibitory neurons in a mouse model for Kleefstra Syndrome using whole-brain clearing.
Current antibody discovery methods lack the scale to fully screen the antibody repertoire of transgenic mouse models.
extractions.method: Novel combinatorial barcoding approach for high-throughput single cell BCR and whole transcriptome profiling of transgenic mouse samples.
extractions.finding: Profiled over 1 million cells obtaining hundreds of thousands of paired BCR clonotypes; detected all B cell subpopulations at single cell resolution; found specific VDJ utilization and amino acid motif patterns in expanded clonotypes.
extractions.dataset: Multiple transgenic mice (naive and immunized), >1 million cells
extractions.metric: Paired BCR clonotype count, B cell subpopulation detection
extractions.result: >1 million cells profiled, hundreds of thousands of paired BCR clonotypes

extractions.problem: Current antibody discovery methods lack the scale to fully screen the antibody repertoire of transgenic mouse models.
extractions.method: Novel combinatorial barcoding approach for high-throughput single cell BCR and whole transcriptome profiling of transgenic mouse samples.
extractions.finding: Profiled over 1 million cells obtaining hundreds of thousands of paired BCR clonotypes; detected all B cell subpopulations at single cell resolution; found specific VDJ utilization patterns in expanded clonotypes.
extractions.dataset: Multiple transgenic mice (naive and immunized), >1 million cells
extractions.metric: Paired BCR clonotype count, B cell subpopulation detection
extractions.result: >1 million cells profiled, hundreds of thousands of paired BCR clonotypes

extractions.problem: Mechanisms driving clonal dominance and ineffective hematopoiesis in PNH with bone marrow failure remain unclear; no PNH-specific mutations have been conclusively identified.
extractions.method: Patient-derived iPSC modeling (PNH-iPSCs and matched wild-type N-iPSCs), CRISPR/Cas9 PIGA correction, hematopoietic differentiation, and whole-genome sequencing (WGS).
extractions.finding: PIGA correction failed to fully restore normal hematopoiesis; WGS identified PNH clone-specific somatic mutations (including DNMT3A, ASXL1 and novel genes) in all cases, supporting genetic heterogeneity of PNH-BMF.
extractions.dataset: 5 PNH patients with bone marrow failure, matched iPSC lines
extractions.metric: Hematopoietic differentiation/proliferation capacity, somatic mutation identification
extractions.baseline: Matched wild-type N-iPSCs from same patients
extractions.result: Multiple PNH clone-specific somatic mutations identified in all 4 sequenced patients; one patient progressed to MDS

extractions.problem: Translating in vitro buccal permeation data to in vivo and whole-body exposure predictions.
Replacing expensive induction-loop traffic sensors with cell phone-based sensors while overcoming public privacy opt-in barriers.
extractions.method: Cell phone radio signal detection as virtual sensors for traffic light controllers, with careful controller training to work with low opt-in rates.
extractions.finding: Even with a small percentage of opted-in users, the new traffic controllers provide large benefits to all drivers.
extractions.result: Large benefits with small percentage of opted-in users

extractions.problem: Making rule-based modeling and simulation accessible to beginners through an intuitive graphical interface within Virtual Cell.
Modeling the effect of UBE3A on dendritic spine morphogenesis using rule-based modeling.
Efficient handover measurement in small-cell networks to reduce processing time and overhead by distinguishing intra-cell and inter-cell handovers.
extractions.method: Virtual cell synchronization signal (VCSS) with hierarchical macro-cell ID and virtual-cell ID using 3D beamforming in LTE-based small-cell networks.
extractions.finding: The proposed VCSS-based technique significantly reduces handover processing time and overhead by distinguishing between intra-cell and inter-cell handovers.
extractions.dataset: Two LTE-based small-cell network deployment scenarios (simulation)
extractions.result: Significant reduction in processing time and overhead

extractions.problem: Extending rule-based modeling to the spatial domain with Virtual Cell (VCELL).
Isolating CTC clusters from background leukocytes without shear stress damage, which existing physical-based methods cannot avoid.
extractions.method: Optically-induced-dielectrophoresis (ODEP)-based virtual cell microfilters in a T-shaped microfluidic chip, using controllable light patterns as virtual electrodes.
extractions.finding: Established a high-purity CTC cluster isolation method with 70.1% recovery and 60.8% purity, avoiding shear stress damage to isolated clusters.
extractions.dataset: CTC clusters (2-13 cells) mixed with background leukocytes

extractions.problem: Cell biophysics modeling and simulation with Virtual Cell software.
In ultra-dense networks, overlapping user-centric virtual cells make multi-dimensional cooperation difficult due to complex interference and coupled resource dimensions.
extractions.method: Graph-based virtual cell forming with Pairwise Maximum Weights (PMW) clustering, using edge weight design capturing interference-fair power allocation, MU-MIMO, and cooperative transmission.
extractions.finding: The proposed algorithm effectively increases system performance at low complexity compared to reference schemes.
extractions.result: Improved system performance at low complexity

extractions.problem: Predicting binding and distribution of chemicals in in vitro toxicity assays using virtual cell models.
Traditional iterative search water filling (ISWF) for power allocation in ultra-dense networks requires too many iterations.
extractions.method: Low-complexity iterative water filling (LAWF) and sorting water filling (SWF) algorithms for virtual cell power allocation in UDN.
extractions.finding: LAWF and SWF achieve better sum rates than ISWF with fewer iterations; SWF performance increases with UDN deployment density.
extractions.result: LAWF and SWF outperform ISWF in sum rate

extractions.problem: Virtual cell power allocation and interference management in ultra-dense networks.
Virtual cell formation for cellular manufacturing systems considering real-life production constraints to improve scheduling flexibility.
extractions.method: Fuzzy c-means clustering approach for machine cell formation using production attributes (processing time, alternative routings, sequence of operation, machine flexibility, capacity, product demand).
extractions.finding: The proposed fuzzy c-means method outperforms existing methods in many instances for cell formation across 15 varied-size problems from literature.
extractions.dataset: 15 cases of different size problems from open literature
extractions.result: Better performance than existing methods in many instances

extractions.problem: Mitigating both intra-virtual-cell and inter-virtual-cell interference in uplink cooperative decoding systems.
extractions.method: Resource allocation technique using interference graphs between base stations at virtual cell edges, with frequency band allocation based on user counts per base station.
extractions.finding: The proposed scheme decreases the number of unsatisfied users whose rate falls below guaranteed bit rates in a mmWave system.
extractions.dataset: Simulated mmWave uplink virtual cell system
extractions.metric: Number of unsatisfied users (rate below guaranteed bit rate)
extractions.result: Fewer unsatisfied users at 128/256/512 kbps guaranteed rates

extractions.problem: Achieving high power density in power electronic transformers using multi-cell rectifier topologies.
extractions.method: ISOS multi-cell full-wave rectifier with GaN triangular current mode (TCM) virtual capacitor converters for voltage sharing without sensors or feedback control.
extractions.finding: Prototype demonstrates potential for over 96% efficiency and over 3.0 W/cm³ power density, enabling high power density power electronic transformers.
extractions.dataset: AC 100V, 450W prototype with 3 cell converters and 2 VCCs

extractions.problem: Building a virtual work environment to support design and planning of human-robot collaborative assembly cells.
extractions.method: VR interface-based motion simulation in virtual environment for interactive evaluation and improvement of assembly process plans and equipment layouts.

extractions.problem: Isolating CTC clusters from background leukocytes without shear stress damage, which existing physical-based methods cannot avoid.
extractions.method: Optically-induced-dielectrophoresis (ODEP)-based virtual cell microfilters in a T-shaped microfluidic chip, using controllable light patterns as virtual electrodes.
extractions.finding: Established a high-purity CTC cluster isolation method with 70.1% recovery and 60.8% purity, avoiding shear stress damage to isolated clusters.
extractions.dataset: CTC clusters (2-13 cells) mixed with background leukocytes

extractions.problem: Non-invasive virtual cell labeling by coupling single-cell innate fluorescent signatures with spatial information.
Frequent adjustment of virtual cell scheduling plans due to new order arrivals causes production instability.
extractions.method: Insertion rescheduling strategy based on slack time subdivision (idle, partial relaxation, total relaxation) solved by backtracking search algorithm with non-dominated sorting.
extractions.finding: The proposed rescheduling strategy minimizes maximum completion time and instability coefficient, verified through numerical example.

extractions.problem: Virtual Cell modeling and simulation software development.
The concept and development of the virtual cell.
The concept and development of the virtual cell.
Traditional in-person cell culture labs face accessibility, cost, and infrastructure barriers; virtual alternatives lack decision-driven interactivity.
extractions.method: Interactive 3D browser-based virtual mammalian cell culture simulation with independent decision-making and technical feedback, assessed via qualitative surveys.
extractions.finding: 73% of students found clear real-world connections, 57% agreed it aided cell culture understanding, and over half found the experience realistic and requiring critical thinking.
extractions.dataset: Upper-level undergraduate/graduate biotechnology students (n=87+96 post-lab, n=73+74 end-of-course)

extractions.problem: Computationally modeling the complex process of amoeboid cell crawling including cell polarization, shape deformation, and locomotion.
extractions.method: Phase field approach coupled with stochastic partial differential equations for intracellular polarization, enabling simultaneous calculation of shape deformations and cell locomotion.
extractions.finding: The phase field model successfully integrates interior polarization dynamics with cell shape changes and locomotion for modeling chemotaxis.

extractions.problem: Virtual Cell modeling and simulation environment development.
Energy-efficient data propagation in wireless sensor networks with a single mobile sink to extend network lifetime.
extractions.method: Virtual cell-based data dissemination with cluster heads aggregating data from member nodes and relaying to a mobile sink following a specific route pattern.
extractions.finding: The approach reduces energy consumption, minimizes packet loss, and enhances network lifetime compared to existing routing protocols.
extractions.dataset: Simulated wireless sensor network with single mobile sink
extractions.metric: Energy consumption, packet loss, network lifetime, data delivery speed
extractions.result: Decreased energy consumption, improved network lifetime and data delivery

extractions.problem: Generating large-scale Perturb-seq datasets to power virtual cell AI models.
Accelerating AI model development for virtual cell modeling.
Accelerating AI model development for virtual cell modeling.
